heading: Application to Medicare Advantage program and related managed care programs
label: 1395w–131 Application to Medicare Advantage program and related managed care programs

children.heading: Special rules relating to offering of qualified prescription drug coverage
children.label: a Special rules relating to offering of qualified prescription drug coverage
A
children.text: may not offer an MA plan described in in an area unless either that plan (or another MA plan offered by the organization in that same service area) includes required prescription drug coverage (as defined in paragraph (2)); andsection 1395w–21(a)(2)(A) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: under an MSA plan; or

children.label: ii
children.text: under another MA plan unless such drug coverage under such other plan provides qualified prescription drug coverage and unless the requirements of this section with respect to such coverage are met.

children.label: A
children.text: basic prescription drug coverage; or

children.label: B
children.text: qualified prescription drug coverage that provides supplemental prescription drug coverage, so long as there is no MA monthly supplemental beneficiary premium applied under the plan (due to the application of a credit against such premium of a rebate under ).section 1395w–24(b)(1)(C) of this title

children.heading: Application of default enrollment rules
children.label: b Application of default enrollment rules

children.heading: Seamless continuation
children.label: 1 Seamless continuation
children.text: In applying , an individual who is enrolled in a health benefits plan shall not be considered to have been deemed to make an election into an MA–PD plan unless such health benefits plan provides any prescription drug coverage.section 1395w–21(c)(3)(A)(ii) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: for purposes of the election as of , the MA plan provided as of , any prescription drug coverage; orJanuary 1, 2006December 31, 2005

children.label: B
children.text: for periods after , such MA plan is an MA–PD plan.January 1, 2006

children.heading: Discontinuance of MA–PD election during first year of eligibility
children.label: 3 Discontinuance of MA–PD election during first year of eligibility
children.text: In applying the second sentence of in the case of an individual who is electing to discontinue enrollment in an MA–PD plan, the individual shall be permitted to enroll in a prescription drug plan under part D at the time of the election of coverage under the original medicare fee-for-service program.section 1395w–21(e)(4) of this title

children.heading: Rules regarding enrollees in MA plans not providing qualified prescription drug coverage
children.label: 4 Rules regarding enrollees in MA plans not providing qualified prescription drug coverage
i
children.text: the individual is deemed to have elected the original medicare fee-for-service program option, unless the individual affirmatively elects to enroll in an MA–PD plan; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of such a deemed election, the disenrollment shall be treated as an involuntary termination of the MA plan described in subparagraph (B)(ii) of for purposes of applying such section.section 1395ss(s)(3) of this title

children.heading: Application of part D rules for prescription drug coverage
children.label: c Application of part D rules for prescription drug coverage

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided, the provisions of this part shall apply under part C with respect to prescription drug coverage provided under MA–PD plans in lieu of the other provisions of part C that would apply to such coverage under such plans.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: 2 Waiver
children.text: The Secretary shall waive the provisions referred to in paragraph (1) to the extent the Secretary determines that such provisions duplicate, or are in conflict with, provisions otherwise applicable to the organization or plan under part C or as may be necessary in order to improve coordination of this part with the benefits under this part.

children.heading: Treatment of MA owned and operated pharmacies
children.label: 3 Treatment of MA owned and operated pharmacies
children.text: The Secretary may waive the requirement of in the case of an MA–PD plan that provides access (other than mail order) to qualified prescription drug coverage through pharmacies owned and operated by the MA organization, if the Secretary determines that the organization’s pharmacy network is sufficient to provide comparable access for enrollees under the plan.section 1395w–104(b)(1)(C) of this title

children.heading: Special rules for private fee-for-service plans that offer prescription drug coverage
children.label: d Special rules for private fee-for-service plans that offer prescription drug coverage

children.heading: Requirements regarding negotiated prices
children.label: 1 Requirements regarding negotiated prices
children.text: Subsections (a)(1) and (d)(1) of and shall not be construed to require the plan to provide negotiated prices (described in subsection (d)(1)(B) of such section), but shall apply to the extent the plan does so.section 1395w–102 of this titlesection 1395w–104(b)(2)(A) of this title

children.heading: Modification of pharmacy access standard and disclosure requirement
children.label: 2 Modification of pharmacy access standard and disclosure requirement
children.text: If the plan provides coverage for drugs purchased from all pharmacies, without charging additional cost-sharing, and without regard to whether they are participating pharmacies in a network or have entered into contracts or agreements with pharmacies to provide drugs to enrollees covered by the plan, subsections (b)(1)(C) and (k) of shall not apply to the plan.section 1395w–104 of this title

children.heading: Drug utilization management program and medication therapy management program not required
children.label: 3 Drug utilization management program and medication therapy management program not required
children.text: The requirements of subparagraphs (A) and (C) of shall not apply to the plan.section 1395w–104(c)(1) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: bases such amount on the Secretary’s estimate of the amount of such payments that would be payable if the plan were an MA–PD plan described in and the previous provisions of this subsection did not apply; andsection 1395w–21(a)(2)(A)(i) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: takes into account the average reinsurance payments made under for populations of similar risk under MA–PD plans described in such section.section 1395w–115(b) of this title

children.heading: Exemption from risk corridor provisions
children.label: 5 Exemption from risk corridor provisions
children.text: The provisions of shall not apply.section 1395w–115(e) of this title

children.heading: Exemption from negotiations
children.label: 6 Exemption from negotiations
children.text: Subsections (d) and (e)(2)(C) of shall not apply and the provisions of prohibiting the review, approval, or disapproval of amounts described in such section shall apply to the proposed bid and terms and conditions described in .section 1395w–111 of this titlesection 1395w–24(a)(5)(B) of this titlesection 1395w–111(d) of this title

children.heading: Treatment of incurred costs without regard to formulary
children.label: 7 Treatment of incurred costs without regard to formulary
children.text: The exclusion of costs incurred for covered part D drugs which are not included (or treated as being included) in a plan’s formulary under shall not apply insofar as the plan does not utilize a formulary.section 1395w–102(b)(4)(C)(i) of this title

children.heading: Application to reasonable cost reimbursement contractors
children.label: e Application to reasonable cost reimbursement contractors

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (2) and (3) and rules established by the Secretary, in the case of an organization that is providing benefits under a reasonable cost reimbursement contract under and that elects to provide qualified prescription drug coverage to a part D eligible individual who is enrolled under such a contract, the provisions of this part (and related provisions of part C) shall apply to the provision of such coverage to such enrollee in the same manner as such provisions apply to the provision of such coverage under an MA–PD local plan described in and coverage under such a contract that so provides qualified prescription drug coverage shall be deemed to be an MA–PD local plan.section 1395mm(h) of this titlesection 1395–21(a)(2)(A)(i) of this title

children.heading: Limitation on enrollment
children.label: 2 Limitation on enrollment
children.text: In applying paragraph (1), the organization may not enroll part D eligible individuals who are not enrolled under the reasonable cost reimbursement contract involved.

children.heading: Bids not included in determining national average monthly bid amount
children.label: 3 Bids not included in determining national average monthly bid amount
children.text: The bid of an organization offering prescription drug coverage under this subsection shall not be taken into account in computing the national average monthly bid amount and low-income benchmark premium amount under this part.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraphs (2) and (3) and rules established by the Secretary, in the case of a PACE program under that elects to provide qualified prescription drug coverage to a part D eligible individual who is enrolled under such program, the provisions of this part (and related provisions of part C) shall apply to the provision of such coverage to such enrollee in a manner that is similar to the manner in which such provisions apply to the provision of such coverage under an MA–PD local plan described in and a PACE program that so provides such coverage may be deemed to be an MA–PD local plan.section 1395eee of this titlesection 1395w–21(a)(2)(A)(ii) of this title

children.heading: Limitation on enrollment
children.label: 2 Limitation on enrollment
children.text: In applying paragraph (1), the organization may not enroll part D eligible individuals who are not enrolled under the PACE program involved.

children.heading: Bids not included in determining standardized bid amount
children.label: 3 Bids not included in determining standardized bid amount
children.text: The bid of an organization offering prescription drug coverage under this subsection is not be taken into account in computing any average benchmark bid amount and low-income benchmark premium amount under this part.
